steps.title: Compare the two texts
steps.content: Text 1 describes the conventional wisdom: because many phytoplankton species compete for the same resources near ocean surfaces, one species should outcompete the others. Text 2 gives Markov's team's explanation for why that expectation may be wrong. Their microscopic size makes the organisms far apart from each other and makes direct interaction difficult.

steps.title: Match Markov's response
steps.content:
• Choice B - 'By asserting that it fails to recognize that routine replenishment of ocean nutrients prevents competition between phytoplankton species': Text 2 does not mention nutrient replenishment.
  • Choice C - 'By suggesting that their own findings help clarify how phytoplankton species are able to compete with larger organisms': The issue is competition among phytoplankton species, not competition with larger organisms.
  • Choice D - 'By recommending that more ecologists focus their research on how competition among phytoplankton species is increased with water density': Text 2 says dense water makes movement and interaction harder, so this reverses the finding.
The remaining choice matches Markov's likely response: the conventional wisdom rests on a mistaken assumption about direct competition among phytoplankton. The answer is A.
